title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
summary: Headroom is an open-source tool built by a Netflix engineer that compresses AI agent inputs, tool outputs, code files, and RAG results before sending them to an LLM. It reduces token usage by 60-95% using locally trained compression models, with zero token cost for compression and on-demand full-text retrieval via breadcrumb hashes.
writeup: This video covers Headroom, an open-source tool from a Netflix engineer that compresses LLM inputs (tool outputs, code files, and RAG results) before they reach the model, cutting token usage by 60-95%. It matters because input tokens drive agent costs, and lower token counts translate directly into cheaper runs. Key takeaways: Headroom uses content-type-aware compressors and locally trained Kompress-based models to compress at zero token cost, and a breadcrumb hash mechanism lets the full originals be retrieved on demand.
